title: AI-Driven Memory Supercycle: Bigger, Longer, and a Shortage that is Harder to Solve
title_translation: AI驱动的存储超级周期：规模更大、持续更长、短缺更难缓解

paragraphs.source: For those who have lived through multiple memory cycles, the central question when it comes to this supercycle is the same: when will this cycle peak? It is natural that both investors and the supply chain remain cautious, particularly as memory stocks rally sharply over short periods. In our view, however, while there are clear similarities to prior cycles, this supercycle is shaping up to be both larger and longer in duration, driven by dynamics that are very much unique to this cycle.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: For those who have lived through multiple memory cycles, the central question when it comes to this supercycle is the same: *when will this cycle peak?* It is natural that both investors and the supply chain remain cautious, particularly as memory stocks rally sharply over short periods. In our view, however, while there are clear similarities to prior cycles, this supercycle is shaping up to be both larger and longer in duration, driven by dynamics that are very much unique to this cycle.

pro.text: 对于那些经历过多轮存储周期的业内人士而言，面对当前的超级周期，核心疑问依然如故：本轮周期何时见顶？ 投资者与供应链保持谨慎实属正常，尤其是在存储板块短期内急剧飙升的背景下。然而在我们看来，尽管本轮周期与以往有明显的相似之处，但在其独有动力的驱动下，这场超级周期的规模必将更为庞大，持续时间也将更为长久。

applied_terms: Maintain a strict distinction between 'wafer capacity' (晶圆产能) and 'bit output' (比特产出).
Manufacturing terms must sound native: 'execution friction' (执行摩擦/落地阻力), 'process windows' (工艺窗口), 'yields deteriorate' (良率下降/爬坡期良率折损).

understanding.role_in_article: Delivers the core thesis: explaining exactly why the AI-driven supercycle is unique, larger, and longer due to specific structural manufacturing constraints.
understanding.relation_to_previous: Builds on the baseline of tight supply by introducing the unique, exacerbating variables of the AI era (HBM wafer drain).
understanding.relation_to_next: Establishes the massive upstream supply constraints that inevitably cause the downstream price shocks detailed in the following section.
key_points: First-order impact: Reallocating wafers to HBM directly starves commodity DRAM production.
Second-order impact: HBM production suffers a massive bit output penalty, yielding 3x to 4x fewer bits per wafer than commodity DRAM.
Node migration introduces execution friction, with new tool sets and process window re-optimization causing temporary yield and capacity declines.

issues.description: 译文在绝大多数段落（如段落 11、18、23、24 等）将 Commodity DRAM 译为“大宗DRAM”，这直接违反了 Translation Guidelines 中明确要求的“Translate 'Commodity DRAM' as '标准型 DRAM'”，且与译文自身在段落 32、35 中使用的“标准型 DRAM”前后不一。此外，“大宗”与“DRAM”之间缺失了标准的中西文空格。
译文中存在多处多余的空格或空格位置错误，如“仅为 约 123kwspm”、“增至 约 331kwspm”、“实现了 超过约 2.7 倍”。
译文处理为“晶圆制造设备（WFE）资本支出（WFE capex）预测”，出现了重复的英文缩写括注。
段落 28、35、39 中，“12层HBM3E”、“8层或12层堆叠”等数字与汉字之间缺失标准的中西文空格。
将 ASIC 直接完全汉化为“专用集成电路”，而同级别的 GPU 却保留了英文缩写。

paragraphs.source: This magnitude of shortage is rare in the modern history of the memory industry, both in terms of its severity and its duration. Even during the 2017–2018 memory supercycle, the supply–demand gap was closer to the mid-single-digit range, making the current environment meaningfully tighter by comparison. Moreover, this shortage is likely to persist for far longer than the prior upcycles discussed above.

pro.text: 无论从严重程度还是持续时间来看，这种量级的短缺在存储行业的现代史上都极为罕见。即使在 2017 至 2018 年的存储超级周期期间，供需缺口也仅接近中个位数区间；相比之下，当前的市场环境明显更为吃紧。此外，本轮短缺的持续时间极有可能远超前文探讨的历次上行周期。

paragraphs.source: First is the accelerating HBM/DRAM demand and HBM’s rapidly expanding capacity. The surge in compute demand is translating into a sharp increase in memory requirements over a very short period, driven by DRAM demand across the data center. This includes DDDR/LPDDR/HBM in AI servers and DDR in general-purpose servers. As a result, demand has risen meaningfully across both commodity DRAM and HBM, which both obviously will utilize a lot of industry’s capacity.

pro.text: 首先是 HBM 与 DRAM 需求的加速增长，以及 HBM 产能的迅速扩张。算力需求的激增正转化为短期内存储需求的急剧攀升，这主要受整个数据中心 DRAM 需求的驱动。这包括 AI 服务器中的 DDR/LPDDR/HBM，以及通用服务器中的 DDR。因此，大宗DRAM 和 HBM 的需求均出现显著增长，这两者显然都将消耗大量行业产能。

paragraphs.source: Beginning around 2023, we have observed a clear and steadily accelerating trend in HBM demand, accompanied by increasingly aggressive responses from memory suppliers to expand HBM production capacity and step up investment to capture this rapidly growing compute demand. Exiting 2023, the three major memory suppliers had dedicated only ~123kwspm of wafer capacity to HBM. By year-end 2025, this figure had increased to ~331kwspm, representing more than a ~2.7× expansion in wafer capacity allocated to HBM over just two years.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Beginning around 2023, we have observed a clear and steadily accelerating trend in HBM demand, accompanied by increasingly aggressive responses from memory suppliers to expand HBM production capacity and step up investment to capture this rapidly growing compute demand. Exiting 2023, the three major memory suppliers had dedicated only **~123kwspm** of wafer capacity to HBM. By **year-end 2025**, this figure had increased to **~331kwspm,** representing **more than a ~2.7× expansion** in wafer capacity allocated to HBM over just two years.

Exiting 2023, the three major memory suppliers had dedicated only ~123kwspm of wafer capacity to HBM. By year-end 2025, this figure had increased to ~331kwspm, representing more than a ~2.7× expansion in wafer capacity allocated to HBM over just two years.

pro.text: 大约从 2023 年开始，我们观察到 HBM 需求呈现出清晰且稳步加速的趋势。与此同时，存储供应商的反应也愈发激进，纷纷扩充 HBM 产能并加大投资，以期充分把握这一快速增长的算力需求。2023 年底时，三大存储供应商分配给 HBM 的晶圆产能仅约为 123kwspm。到2025 年底，这一数字将增至约 331kwspm，这意味着在短短两年内，分配给 HBM 的晶圆产能实现了超过约 2.7 倍的扩张。

paragraphs.source: And the capacity expansion in HBM is far from complete. pro.text: 而且，HBM 的产能扩张还远未结束。我们认为，新一轮产能扩张已在进行中，预计到 2026 年底，HBM 晶圆产能将达到约 473kwspm，到 2027 年底将达到约 668kwspm。这意味着在未来两年内，HBM 晶圆产能将（再次）实现两倍以上的扩张；换言之，三大存储厂商的 HBM 晶圆产能将在四年内增长至 5 倍。

paragraphs.source: The impact of materially expanding HBM capacity is unambiguous. When we examine HBM’s wafer intensity within overall DRAM production, we find that HBM wafer capacity as a share of total DRAM wafer capacity at Samsung, SK hynix, and Micron was below 5% in 2022, but has already increased to approximately 20% by year-end 2025. By the end of 2027, we estimate this figure will reach ~35%. This implies that HBM alone will consume more than one-third of the combined DRAM wafer capacity across the three suppliers, directly displacing capacity that would otherwise be available for commodity DRAM production.

paragraphs.source: One may ask why memory makers are moving so aggressively to expand HBM capacity. We believe the rationale is twofold. First, HBM is increasingly viewed as a structural and sustainable growth engine that extends well beyond traditional memory end markets such as PCs, mobile, and automotive. This is reinforced by the continued increase in HBM intensity within AI servers (both GPU and ASIC), where HBM remains indispensable for both model training and inference. As a result, rising HBM content per AI server should meaningfully drive incremental demand going forward.

As a result, rising HBM content per AI server should meaningfully drive incremental demand going forward.

pro.text: 人们不禁要问，存储制造商为何如此激进地扩张 HBM 产能。我们认为背后有双重驱动因素。首先，HBM 愈发被视为一种结构性且可持续的增长引擎，其应用边界已远超 PC、移动设备和汽车等传统存储终端市场。AI 服务器（包括 GPU 和 ASIC）中 HBM 搭载量的持续攀升进一步印证了这一点，无论是在模型训练还是推理环节，HBM 都不可或缺。因此，单台 AI 服务器 HBM 容量的提升必将大幅拉动未来的增量需求。

paragraphs.source: Second, HBM’s complex front-end and back-end manufacturing requirements enable greater product differentiation relative to commodity DRAM. Performance characteristics such as pin speed, power efficiency, thermal capability, and packaging integration vary meaningfully by supplier compared to conventional DRAM, allowing memory makers to distinguish their products more clearly. This differentiation creates room for pricing power, incremental margin capture, and market share gain, in contrast to commodity DRAM where competition is driven largely by cost and scale. SK hynix’ success in both HBM3 and HBM3E should be the best example for this.

pro.text: 其次，相较于大宗DRAM，HBM 复杂的前道与后道制造要求赋予了产品更大的差异化空间。与传统 DRAM 相比，不同供应商在引脚速度、能效、散热能力以及封装集成度等性能特征上存在显著差异，这使得存储制造商能够更清晰地凸显自身产品优势。这种差异化为获取定价权、攫取增量利润率以及抢占市场份额创造了空间，这与主要依靠成本和规模驱动竞争的大宗DRAM 截然不同。SK海力士在 HBM3 和 HBM3E 上的成功便是最佳佐证。

paragraphs.source: Yet, such a significant expansion of HBM capacity carries meaningful cost and strategic challenges. As memory manufacturers increasingly allocate both existing and incremental wafer capacity to HBM, they not only materially constrain capacity available to traditional DRAM end markets but also reduce overall DRAM bit output due to HBM’s lower bit conversion rate. The result is an even tighter commodity DRAM supply environment, even as total industry wafer capacity expands—reinforcing supply pressure across the broader DRAM market.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Yet, such a significant expansion of HBM capacity carries meaningful cost and strategic challenges. As memory manufacturers increasingly allocate both existing and incremental wafer capacity to HBM, they not only materially constrain capacity available to traditional DRAM end markets but also reduce overall DRAM bit output due to HBM’s lower bit conversion rate. The result is an even tighter commodity DRAM supply environment, even as total industry wafer capacity expands—reinforcing supply pressure across the broader DRAM market.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Yet, such a significant expansion of HBM capacity carries meaningful cost and strategic challenges. As memory manufacturers increasingly allocate both existing and incremental wafer capacity to HBM, they not only materially constrain capacity available to traditional DRAM end markets but also reduce overall DRAM bit output due to HBM’s lower bit conversion rate. The result is an even tighter commodity DRAM supply environment, even as total industry wafer capacity expands—reinforcing supply pressure across the broader DRAM market.

pro.text: 然而，如此大规模的 HBM 产能扩张也带来了不容忽视的成本与战略挑战。随着存储制造商不断将现有及新增晶圆产能向 HBM 倾斜，他们不仅严重挤压了传统 DRAM 终端市场的可用产能，还因 HBM 较低的位元转换率拉低了整体 DRAM 的比特产出。其结果是，即便全行业的晶圆总产能不断扩张，大宗DRAM 的供应环境却愈发吃紧，进一步加剧了整个 DRAM 市场的供应压力。

Second-order impact: HBM production suffers a massive bit output penalty, yielding 3x to 4x fewer bits per wafer than commodity DRAM.
Node migration introduces execution friction, with new tool sets and process window re-optimization causing temporary yield and capacity declines.

paragraphs.source: The first-order impact requires little explanation: reallocating wafer capacity to HBM directly reduces the number of wafers available for commodity DRAM production. However, the second-order effect is equally important and sometimes underappreciated. A wafer dedicated to commodity DRAM typically delivers roughly 3× the bit output of a wafer dedicated to HBM in the case of HBM3E 12-Hi. Based on our estimates, this gap is likely to widen to nearly 4× as the industry transitions to HBM4 later this year and potentially even more in HBM4E the following year.

pro.text: 一阶影响不言而喻：将晶圆产能重新分配给 HBM，会直接减少可用于生产标准型 DRAM 的晶圆数量。然而，二阶效应同样重要，却往往被低估。以 HBM3E 12-Hi（12 层堆叠）为例，专门用于生产标准型 DRAM 的晶圆，其比特产出通常约为 HBM 专属晶圆的 3 倍。根据我们的测算，随着今年晚些时候行业向 HBM4 过渡，这一差距可能扩大至近 4 倍；到明年推出 HBM4E 时，差距甚至会进一步拉大。

paragraphs.source: HBM uses larger die sizes as each HBM stack consists of multiple large DRAM dies optimized for bandwidth rather than density. HBM’s larger die size is due to the presence of through-silicon vias (TSVs), which require dedicated keep-out zones that reduce usable array area and inflate die size. This materially reduces the number of dies that can be harvested per wafer compared to commodity DRAM dies. HBM, compared to commodity DDR DRAM, also demands much greater cell performance, which dramatically reduces front-end sort yields for HBM wafers.

This materially reduces the number of dies that can be harvested per wafer compared to commodity DRAM dies. pro.text: HBM 采用更大的裸片尺寸，因为每个 HBM 堆叠均由多个大尺寸 DRAM 裸片组成，这些裸片专为带宽而非存储密度进行了优化。HBM 裸片尺寸较大，归因于硅通孔（TSV）的存在。TSV 需要专属的禁布区，这不仅缩减了可用的阵列面积，还导致裸片尺寸膨胀。与大宗DRAM 裸片相比，这大幅减少了单片晶圆上可切割出的合格裸片数量。此外，相较于大宗 DDR DRAM，HBM 对存储单元的性能要求苛刻得多，这导致 HBM 晶圆的前道分选良率一路陡降。

paragraphs.source: Stacking and advanced packaging (primary bonding) yields also meaningfully impact HBM output. Even when individual DRAM die yields are high, the compounded yield across 8-high or 12-high stacks is structurally lower, as a single defective die can compromise the usability of an entire stack. This stands in contrast to commodity DRAM, where each die is sold independently, and yield losses are more localized. Finally, HBM products prioritize bandwidth and power efficiency over maximum density, limiting the degree to which bit scaling can offset these inherent inefficiencies.

pro.text: 堆叠与先进封装（主要指键合）的良率同样对 HBM 产出产生重大影响。即使单个 DRAM 裸片良率很高，8层或12层堆叠的复合良率在结构上依然偏低，因为哪怕只有一个裸片存在缺陷，也会导致整个堆叠失效。这与标准型 DRAM 形成鲜明对比：后者的每个裸片均独立销售，良率折损仅限于单个裸片。最后，HBM 产品将带宽与能效置于最高存储密度之上，因此，通过比特缩放来弥补这些固有低效的空间十分有限。

paragraphs.source: We think what distinguishes this cycle is that the product transition driving demand - HBM - is far more manufacturing-intensive, effectively introducing a form of reverse scaling. Unlike prior computing platform inflections that primarily expanded demand without materially constraining supply, the AI-driven shift toward HBM simultaneously boosts demand while tightening supply. This dynamic is further amplified by rising server DRAM content, including emerging LPDDR5-based products such as SOCAMM and SOCAMM2.

This dynamic is further amplified by rising server DRAM content, including emerging LPDDR5-based products such as SOCAMM and SOCAMM2.

pro.text: 我们认为，本轮周期的独特之处在于，驱动需求的产品迭代——即 HBM——对制造资源的消耗远超以往，实际上引入了一种反向缩放。此前的计算平台变革主要在于扩大需求，并未对供给造成实质性约束；而此次由 AI 驱动的 HBM 转型，在推升需求的同时收紧了供给。随着服务器 DRAM 单机容量的攀升，包括 SOCAMM 与 SOCAMM2 等基于 LPDDR5 的新兴产品不断涌现，这一供需动态被进一步放大。

paragraphs.source: NVIDIA, for example, the transition from Blackwell to Blackwell Ultra and Rubin increases HBM capacity by ~50%, with Rubin Ultra driving a further ~4× jump in system memory from 288 GB to ~1 TB. Similarly, ASICs such as Goole’s TPU v8AX and Amazon’s Trainium3 also migrating from HBM3E 8-Hi to 12-Hi stacks, while AMD’s memory capacity increases materially from ~288 GB in MI350 to ~432 GB in MI400. At a high level, it is not difficult to envision the magnitude of DRAM demand required to support both existing and next-generation AI compute platforms.

pro.text: 以英伟达（NVIDIA）为例，从 Blackwell 向 Blackwell Ultra 及 Rubin 的过渡，使 HBM 容量提升了约 50%；而 Rubin Ultra 更是推动系统内存实现约 4 倍的跨越式增长，从 288 GB 跃升至约 1 TB。同样，谷歌（Google）的张量处理器（TPU）v8AX 与亚马逊（Amazon）的 Trainium3 等 ASIC 芯片，也正从 HBM3E 8层堆叠向 12层堆叠迁移；同时，AMD 的内存容量也实现了大幅增长，从 MI350 的约 288 GB 增至 MI400 的约 432 GB。从宏观层面来看，要支撑现有及下一代 AI 计算平台，所需的 DRAM 需求规模之大已不言而喻。

issues.description: 译文在绝大多数段落（如段落 11、18、23、24 等）将 Commodity DRAM 译为“大宗DRAM”，这直接违反了 Translation Guidelines 中明确要求的“Translate 'Commodity DRAM' as '标准型 DRAM'”，且与译文自身在段落 32、35 中使用的“标准型 DRAM”前后不一。此外，“大宗”与“DRAM”之间缺失了标准的中西文空格。
译文中存在多处多余的空格或空格位置错误，如“仅为 约 123kwspm”、“增至 约 331kwspm”、“实现了 超过约 2.7 倍”。
译文处理为“晶圆制造设备（WFE）资本支出（WFE capex）预测”，出现了重复的英文缩写括注。
段落 28、35、39 中，“12层HBM3E”、“8层或12层堆叠”等数字与汉字之间缺失标准的中西文空格。
将 ASIC 直接完全汉化为“专用集成电路”，而同级别的 GPU 却保留了英文缩写。

paragraphs.source: We estimate that HBM demand alone will account for more than 10% of total DRAM demand by the end of 2027, layered on top of the already substantial server DRAM contribution, which is about 40% of total DRAM end market. Given the DRAM content increases discussed earlier and robust demand across both AI and general-purpose servers, we believe the importance of servers within the overall DRAM end market will continue to increase over the next few years, and is already seen as a top priority for memory suppliers over other categories.

pro.text: 我们预计，到 2027 年底，单是 HBM 需求就将占据 DRAM 总需求的 10% 以上。这还需叠加本就十分可观的服务器 DRAM 份额（约占 DRAM 终端市场总量的 40%）。鉴于前文探讨的 DRAM 单机容量增长，加上 AI 与通用服务器双双展现强劲需求，我们认为未来几年内，服务器在整体 DRAM 终端市场中的重要性必将持续攀升。存储供应商也早已将其视为力压其他品类的首要战略重心。

paragraphs.source: The HBM–DRAM conundrum becomes even more complex when factoring in market reactions to the worsening DRAM shortage driven by HBM capacity reallocation. As commodity DRAM supply has tightened materially due to HBM expansion, pricing and margins have surged over the past several quarters. Commodity DRAM margins are now on par with—or in many cases exceeding—HBM margins since late 4Q, a trend we believe will persist over the next few quarters. This is particularly the case for Samsung, which has lower margin in producing HBM. We first highlighted this development in our Core Research numerous times last year.

Commodity DRAM margins are now on par with—or in many cases exceeding—HBM margins since late 4Q, a trend we believe will persist over the next few quarters. This is particularly the case for Samsung, which has lower margin in producing HBM. We first highlighted this development in our [Core Research](https://semianalysis.com/core-research/) numerous times last year.

pro.text: 若将市场对 DRAM 短缺加剧（由 HBM 产能腾挪所致）的反应纳入考量，HBM 与 DRAM 之间的博弈困局便愈发错综复杂。由于 HBM 扩产导致大宗DRAM 供应显著收紧，其价格与利润率在过去几个季度一路狂飙。自四季度末以来，大宗DRAM 的利润率已与 HBM 齐平，甚至在诸多情况下实现了反超。我们认为这一趋势将在未来几个季度延续。三星的情况尤为典型，其 HBM 生产利润率相对较低。去年，我们已在 Core Research 中多次率先预警这一动向。

paragraphs.source: We view this as a significant and crucial challenge for memory suppliers. Given the combination of an attractive margin outlook and superior bit output from manufacturing and selling commodity DRAM, alongside the increased manufacturing complexity associated with HBM4, some suppliers may adopt a more deliberate approach in 2026—carefully balancing HBM capacity expansion against conventional DRAM production. This dynamic is likely to be more pronounced for suppliers that perceive their near-term ability to gain HBM share to be constrained by product competitiveness and aim to exploit commodity DRAM to maintain it memory business performance.

This dynamic is likely to be more pronounced for suppliers that perceive their near-term ability to gain HBM share to be constrained by product competitiveness and aim to exploit commodity DRAM to maintain it memory business performance.

This dynamic is likely to be more pronounced for suppliers that perceive their near-term ability to gain HBM share to be constrained by product competitiveness and aim to exploit commodity DRAM to maintain it memory business performance.

pro.text: 我们认为，这给存储供应商带来了重大且关键的挑战。一方面，制造与销售大宗DRAM 兼具诱人的利润率前景与更高的比特产出；另一方面，HBM4 的制造复杂度日益攀升。双重因素交织下，部分供应商在 2026 年或将采取更为审慎的策略，在 HBM 扩产与传统 DRAM 生产之间谨慎走钢丝。对于那些自认短期内受限于产品竞争力、难以夺取 HBM 份额的供应商而言，这一动向势必更为显著，他们将试图充分榨取大宗DRAM 的红利，借此维持其存储业务的业绩表现。

paragraphs.source: One major contributor to the current shortage that we have not yet discussed in depth is limited cleanroom availability. Following the memory upcycle during the pandemic, memory suppliers adopted a more conservative approach to capex and capacity expansion. This caution resulted in an underinvestment backdrop entering 2025 and extending into 2026. As demand has continued to accelerate, memory suppliers are now constrained by insufficient cleanroom capacity, limiting their ability to respond on the supply side.

paragraphs.source: With these constraints in mind, the central question is how the industry can meaningfully expand bit supply. We currently forecast ~21% bit growth this year and ~19% next year. In our view, the primary driver of this growth will be accelerated node migration to 1b and 1c across the three major memory suppliers. Achieving this growth requires a faster pace of transition to these nodes, which deliver materially higher bit output per wafer than trailing nodes and seem to be t the most effective remaining lever for bit expansion in a wafer-constrained environment.

pro.text: 鉴于上述产能掣肘，当前的核心命题在于：行业究竟如何才能实现位元供应的实质性扩张？我们目前预测，今年全行业的位元增长约为 21%，明年约为 19%。在我们看来，推动这一增长的核心引擎，将是三大存储供应商全面加速向 1b 和 1c 制程节点演进。要达成上述增长目标，必须大幅加快向新节点的过渡步伐。相较于成熟节点，新节点能带来显著更高的单片晶圆比特产出。在晶圆产能受限的大环境下，这似乎已是实现位元扩张最有效的仅存杠杆。

pro.text: 基于此，我们预计从 2025 年第四季度末至 2027 年第四季度，三大存储供应商的 1b 与 1c 合计产能将激增约 80%。具体而言，我们笃定到 2026 年底，三星与 SK海力士基于 1c 制程节点的 DRAM 晶圆产出占比应接近 30%，或至少将目标锚定在这一阈值附近。美光同样有能力将其约 30% 的 DRAM 产能切入 1c（或 1γ节点）。

paragraphs.source: Yet one should note that DRAM node migration introduces meaningful execution friction. New tool sets must be installed, process windows re-optimized, and yields typically deteriorate during the initial ramp phase. As a result, effective wafer capacity and bit output at transitioning fabs often decline for a few quarters before recovering as yields stabilize and learning curves are climbed. The practical implication is that portions of the installed base across all three major memory suppliers will likely experience temporary overall capacity and bit-output disruptions during this transition but a long-term gain in terms of bit output.

pro.text: 然而必须警惕的是，DRAM 的节点演进必然伴随显著的落地阻力。产线必须导入全新设备组，重新调校工艺窗口，且在初期产能爬坡阶段，良率折损几乎不可避免。受此拖累，处于过渡期的晶圆厂往往会面临长达数个季度的有效晶圆产能与比特产出双降，直至良率企稳并跨越学习曲线后方可稳步复苏。这意味着在实际操作层面，三大存储供应商的部分现有产线在过渡期内，极易遭遇整体产能与比特产出的短暂阵痛，但以此换取的是长线比特产出的实质性跃升。
